zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley於1220年出生在英國越可爾吉賽利時，他的父親Simon Warde11217歲，母親Constance Vesci30歲。他於1253年與Margaret Neville結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1266年7月11日在英國越可爾吉文代爾 白 利朋享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley于1220年出生在英国越可爾吉賽利时，他的父亲Simon Warde11217岁，母亲Constance Vesci30岁。他于1253年与Margaret Neville结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1266年7月11日在英国越可爾吉文代爾 白 利朋享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Donal.
Irish: variant of   Ward  .

variants.name: Ward
meanings: English: occupational name for a watchman or guard, from Middle English ward ‘watchman, guard’ (Old English weard, used as both an agent noun and an abstract noun).
English: occupational name from Middle English warde ‘armed guard’ (Old English weard ‘watching, guarding’), with the same meaning as 1 above.

Irish: shortened form of McWard, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Bhaird ‘son of the poet’. The surname occurs throughout Ireland, where three different branches of the family are known as professional poets.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley was born in 1220, in Guiseley, Yorkshire, England, his father, Simon Warde, was 11217 and his mother, Constance Vesci, was 30. He married Margaret Neville in 1253. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 11 July 1266, in Givendale by Ripon, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley est né en 1220, à Guiseley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Simon Warde, avait 11217 ans et sa mère, Constance Vesci, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Neville en 1253. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 11 juillet 1266, à Givendale by Ripon, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley 1220 in Guiseley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Simon Warde 11217 und seine Mutter Constance Vesci 30. Er hat 1253 Margaret Neville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 11. Juli 1266 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Givendale by Ripon, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley è nato il nel 1220 nel Guiseley, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Simon Warde, aveva 11217 anni e il suo madre, Constance Vesci, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Neville nel 1253. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 11 luglio 1266, nel Givendale by Ripon, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley(이)가 1220년 영국 요르크실르 길즈리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon Warde(은)는 11217세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance Vesci(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1253년 Margaret Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1266년 7월 11일 영국 요르크실르 지븐데일 비 리폰에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley nasceu em 1220, em Guiseley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Simon Warde, tinha 11217 anos e sua mãe, Constance Vesci, tinha 30 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William De la Warde of Guiseley родился в 1220 г. в местности гуисели, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Simon Warde было 11217 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance Vesci, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Neville в 1253 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 11 июля 1266 г. в местности гивендеил би рипон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 46 лет.
